pageIntro.title: A nonprofit movement helping children see technology as something they can shape.
pageIntro.description: Free Code Juniors combines accessible teaching, creative project work, and community support so students do not just learn coding concepts, they build belief in their own potential.

mission.title: Our Mission
mission.description: To make computer science education accessible to every child through thoughtful instruction, creative learning experiences, and a nonprofit model centered on inclusion and encouragement.

vision.title: Our Vision
vision.description: A future where every young learner, regardless of background or experience, has the opportunity to become an innovator, engineer, builder, and problem-solver.

whyCoding.title: Why coding matters
paragraphs: Coding gives students more than a technical skill. It teaches logic, persistence, collaboration, and the habit of turning ideas into something real.
When children build games, stories, apps, and prototypes, they discover that technology is not just something they consume. It is something they can create with purpose and imagination.
Those early experiences matter. They help young people see themselves as capable problem-solvers long before they make choices about school, college, or career paths.

whyNonprofit.title: Why Nonprofit education matters to us
paragraphs: A nonprofit model keeps the focus where it belongs: access, community impact, and meaningful student growth.
It allows Free Code Juniors to invest in welcoming learning spaces, volunteer mentorship, and accessible teaching practices instead of optimizing for exclusivity.
That mission-first approach makes it easier to meet families where they are and open doors for more students to participate with confidence.

storyImages.alt: A classroom prepared for collaborative computer science learning.
Students and volunteers gathered during a Free Code Juniors session.

team.title: Student Instructors
team.description: A student-led volunteer structure helps Free Code Juniors stay personal, welcoming, and community-centered.

members.name: Andrew Chen
members.bio: Creates the class materials and gives hands-on coding sessions.

members.name: Zibo Nie
members.bio: Assists with teaching and provides much needed tech support to keep the class moving.
